q.zh: 斯普特尼克一号（Sputnik I）是什么？

options.en: the first artificial satellite, launched by the Soviet Union in 1957
options.zh: 第一颗人造卫星，由苏联于1957年发射

options.en: the Hubble Space Telescope according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 哈勃太空望远镜（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says the former Soviet Union 'launched the first object into space, the satellite Sputnik I' in 1957; it was 'about the size of a basketball' and marked the start of the space race.
explain.zh: 书中说前苏联于1957年「将第一个物体送入太空——人造卫星斯普特尼克一号」；它「约篮球大小」，标志着太空竞赛的开始。

q.en: Which satellite is described as the most famous U.S. astronomical satellite, launched into low-Earth orbit in 1990?
q.zh: 哪颗卫星被描述为美国最著名的天文卫星，于1990年进入近地轨道？

options.en: Telstar according to some versions of the story
options.zh: Telstar（电星）（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says: 'The Hubble Space Telescope is the most famous U.S. astronomical satellite. The ultimate telescope was launched into its low-Earth orbit in 1990.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：「哈勃太空望远镜是美国最著名的天文卫星。这台『终极望远镜』于1990年发射进入近地轨道。」

q.en: In satellite anatomy, what is the 'payload'?
q.zh: 在卫星构造中，「payload」（有效载荷）是什么？

options.en: the rocket that launches it according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 发射它的火箭（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: the tools the satellite needs to do its work, such as cameras and antennas
options.zh: 卫星工作所需的工具，如相机和天线

explain.en: The book says: 'The payload includes whatever tools the satellite needs to perform its work, such as sensitive antennas, high-resolution cameras, and communication electronics.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：「有效载荷包括卫星执行工作所需的任何工具，如灵敏的天线、高分辨率相机和通信电子设备。」

q.en: In a stationary (equatorial) orbit, why does a satellite appear to stay over the same spot on Earth?
q.zh: 在静止（赤道）轨道上，卫星为什么看起来停在地球上空同一位置？

options.en: it orbits at the same speed Earth rotates, taking 24 hours
options.zh: 它以与地球自转相同的速度运行，耗时24小时

options.en: it is attached to Earth according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它附着在地球上（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says satellites 22,300 miles up 'move at the same speed that the Earth is turning... it takes 24 hours for the satellite to circle our planet. The Earth takes 24 hours to spin on its axis, so these satellites appear to stay over the same spot!'
explain.zh: 书中说22,300英里高的卫星「以与地球自转相同的速度运行……绕地球一圈需24小时。地球自转一圈也需24小时，因此这些卫星看起来停在同一位置上空！」

q.en: What does 'downlinking' mean in the book?
q.zh: 书中「downlinking」（下行链路）是什么意思？

options.en: sending signals up to a satellite according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 将信号发送到卫星（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: a satellite sending collected information back to Earth as a radio signal
options.zh: 卫星将收集的信息作为无线电信号发回地球

explain.en: The book says: 'Downlinking is when a satellite converts the information it collects into a radio signal and sends it back to Earth.' Sending signals up is called uplinking.
explain.zh: 书中写道：「下行链路是指卫星将收集的信息转换为无线电信号并发回地球。」向卫星发送信号称为上行链路。

q.en: In satellite anatomy, what does the word 'bus' refer to?
q.zh: 在卫星构造中，「bus」（卫星总线/主体）指的是什么？

options.en: the part that carries the payload and holds everything together
options.zh: 承载有效载荷并将各部分组合在一起的部分

options.en: the satellite's orbit according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 卫星的轨道（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says: 'The bus is the part that carries the payload. The bus holds all the parts together; provides the electrical power... and the power to move the satellite forward.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：「总线是承载有效载荷的部分。总线将所有部件组合在一起；提供电力……以及推动卫星前进的动力。」

q.en: According to the book, how many satellites' signals does GPS use to pinpoint a location?
q.zh: 根据本书，GPS 利用多少颗卫星的信号来定位？

options.en: ten satellites according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 十颗卫星（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says: 'Global Positioning System, or GPS, receives radio signals from three satellites that are used to pinpoint an exact location anywhere.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：「全球定位系统（GPS）接收来自三颗卫星的无线电信号，用于精确定位任何地点。」

q.en: According to the book, about how fast must low-Earth orbit satellites travel to avoid falling back to Earth?
q.zh: 根据本书，近地轨道卫星必须以多快的速度飞行才能避免落回地球？

options.en: about 100 miles per hour according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 约每小时100英里（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states satellites in low-Earth orbit (200–500 miles up) 'must travel at about 17,000 miles per hour (27,358 kph) to keep from falling back to Earth.'
explain.zh: 书中写道，近地轨道（200–500英里高）的卫星「必须以约每小时17,000英里（27,358公里）的速度飞行，才不至于落回地球」。

q.en: Why does the author mention that a fleck of paint dug a pit in a space shuttle window?
q.zh: 作者为什么要提到一小片油漆在航天飞机窗户上砸出一个坑？

options.en: To show that even tiny pieces of space junk can cause serious damage
options.zh: 为了说明即使微小的太空垃圾也能造成严重损害

options.en: To describe how to paint shuttles according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了描述如何给航天飞机喷漆（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Under 'Hunks of Junk!' the book notes a shuttle 'smacked into a fleck of paint that dug a pit in a window' and that debris hits craft at 170,000 mph, showing readers space junk is dangerous.
explain.zh: 在「大块垃圾！」一节，书中提到航天飞机「撞上一小片油漆，在窗户上砸出一个坑」，且碎片以每小时170,000英里的速度撞击航天器，向读者说明太空垃圾很危险。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To argue that satellites are useless according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了论证卫星毫无用处（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To explain what satellites are and how they help people in everyday life
options.zh: 为了解释什么是卫星，以及它们如何在日常生活中帮助人们

explain.en: The book covers natural and artificial satellites, their parts, orbits, and uses (communication, weather, GPS, astronomy), concluding that 'Satellites are clearly here to stay' and change our lives, showing an explanatory purpose.
explain.zh: 本书涵盖天然与人造卫星、构造、轨道及用途（通信、气象、GPS、天文），结论是「卫星显然会持续存在」并改变生活，体现出说明性写作目的。

prompt.en: The book says GPS was first built for the military but now helps people drive and ships navigate. Name one satellite use from the book that you rely on or find useful, and explain why.
prompt.zh: 书中说GPS最初为军用而建，现在帮助人们开车导航、船只航行。说出书中提到你依赖或觉得有用的一项卫星用途，并解释原因。

en: Choose from communication, weather, GPS, Earth observation, or astronomy.
Explain how that satellite type helps people based on the book's description.
zh: 从通信、气象、GPS、对地观测或天文中选择。
根据书中描述解释这类卫星如何帮助人们。
